tool.name: Scop3P
tool.description: fetches post-translational modifications (PTMs) from Scop3P database
tool.command:
set -euo pipefail
scop3ptm '${accession}' \ --format tsv-modifications \ --include-structures-modifications \ --output '$modifications'
#if str($include_peptides) == "true": scop3ptm '${accession}' \ --format tsv-peptides \ --include-peptides-modifications \ --output '$peptides' #end if
#if str($include_modifications) == "true": scop3ptm '${accession}' \ --format tsv-structures \ --include-structures-modifications \ --output '$structures' #end if

validator.message: UniProtKB accession numbers consist of 6 or 10 alphanumerical characters in the format '[OPQ][0-9][A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]|[A-NR-Z][0-9]([A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}[0-9]){1,2}'
UniProtKB accession numbers consist of 6 or 10 alphanumerical characters

param.name: include_peptides
param.label: Peptides
param.help: Include an additional tabular file with peptides and modifications.

param.name: include_modifications
param.label: Modifications
param.help: Include an additional tabular file with structures and modifications.

organization.name: Bio2Byte, Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB)
organization.address: Interuniversity Institute Bioinformatics Brussels, Université Libre de Bruxelles, 1050 Ixelles, Brussels, Belgium

organization.name: CompOmics, Department of Biomolecular Medicine, Ghent University, VIB-UGent Center for Medical Biotechnology, VIB

tool.help:
`Scop3P <https://iomics.ugent.be/scop3p>`_ provides a unique and powerful resource to explore and understand the impact of phospho-sites on human protein structure and function, and can thus serve as a springboard for researchers seeking to analyse and interpret a given phospho-site or phosphoprotein in a structural, biophysical, and biological context.
The resource re-uses public domain data from a variety of leading international resources, including UniProtKB and PDB, but also uses reprocessed mass spectrometry-based phospho-proteomics data from *PRIDE* / *ProteomExchange*, which is in turn globally collected and thus wholly international-driven.
This tool downloads UniProt post-translational modification (PTM) annotations from the `Scop3P REST API <https://iomics.ugent.be/scop3p/swagger-ui/index.html>`_.
**Tool Parameters:**
- **UniProtKB Accession:** The UniProtKB accession number (6 or 10 alphanumerical characters). - **Peptides:** when selected, it gets peptides and modifications. - **Modifications:** when selected, it gets structures and modifications.
**Outputs:**
1. Base tabular file with the modifications list. 2. Optional tabular file with peptides and modifications list (when **Peptides** is selected). 3. Optional tabular file with structures and modifications list (when **Modifications** is selected).
**Scop3P** is part of the `ELIXIR Belgium services <https://www.elixir-belgium.org/services>`_. For any further questions, feedback or suggestions, please send an email to: scop3p.compomics@vib-ugent.be.
